calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Under Cal. Code Of Civ. Proc. Section 1008(A).
Off Calendar May-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DFND SECURITY, INC.'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION UNDER CAL. CODE OF CIV. PROC. 1008(a).

Appearances via Zoom:
Cyndie Chang, Dillon Denio and Geoff Heaton of Duane Morris (213.689.7400, cmchang@duanemorris.com, ddenio@duanemorris.com) for petitioner Crowdstrike, Inc.
Marc Forsythe of Goe Forsythe & Hodges LLP (949.798.2460 mforsythe@goeforlaw.com) for respondent DFND Security, Inc.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Respondent DFND Security, Inc.'s motion for reconsideration is denied. A motion for reconsideration must, inter alia, include "new or different facts, circumstances, or law." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1008, subd. (a).) To the extent DFND makes new arguments on this motion, it does not offer any showing of "diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not presenting the new or different information earlier." (Even Zohar Constr. & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 833.) Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Teckla Clay, CSR 13125 (310.490.4905, tecklapratt@gmail.com) (301/CVA)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-17-2022 Off Calendar

Dfnd Security Inc S Notice Of Amended Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 418.10 (A) (2) And 410.30(C)
Off Calendar Oct-17-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Joseph Thomas, Esq. (949.679.6400; jthomas@twtlaw.com) of Thomas Whitelaw & Kolegraffe LLP for Respondent.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT DFND SECURITY, INC.'S Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 418.10 (A) (2) And 410.30(C).

Respondent DFND SECURITY, INC.'s ("DFND") motion to stay is granted based on the court's inherent powers and comity. (See CCP 128(a)(8).)

The court exercises its discretion to stay this action until the federal court finally decides on the propriety of DFND's federal petition to vacate arbitration award. The federal action was filed and served before this state court action. While the rule of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction does not technically apply because one action is pending in federal court, a stay is still warranted.

"Under the rule of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction, 'when two superior courts have concurrent jurisdiction over the subject matter and all parties involved in litigation, the first to assume jurisdiction has exclusive and continuing jurisdiction over the subject matter and all parties involved until such time as all necessarily related matters have been resolved.'" (Plant Insulation Co. v. Fibreboard Corp. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 781, 786-787.) The purpose of this rule is to prevent contradictory decisions and wasted resources. Those purposes exist here.

The federal court will determine whether it has jurisdiction to entertain DFND's petition to vacate arbitration award. It would be inefficient for this state court action to proceed since the federal court is presently addressing the same issues. If the federal court resolves the jurisdictional issue in Crowdstrike's favor, it may seek to lift the stay of this action.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
